# HLL byte-parity fixtures

fixtures.txt was generated by gen.py against a real redis-server
8.8.0 and is replayed by TestHLLRedisParity. Every C line is a
command with the reply the real server gave; every V line is a hex
snapshot of the value bytes at that point, taken with GET. Because
PFCOUNT writes its cached cardinality back into the value, the
snapshots after PFCOUNT pin the Ertl estimator bit for bit, not just
the envelope layout.

To regenerate:

    redis-server --port 7399 --save '' --appendonly no &
    python3 gen.py 7399 > fixtures.txt
    redis-cli -p 7399 shutdown nosave

Regenerating against a different Redis version can legitimately
change the bytes (error texts moved between 8.0 and 8.8 already); if
it does, the sqlo1 implementation follows the version this file was
generated with, currently 8.8.0.
